title: oxalis corniculata
intro: Oxalis corniculata, also known as the wood sorrel or creeping wood sorrel, is a perennial herb that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Oxalis corniculata include its potential to reduce inflammation, act as an antioxidant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and wounds. Oxalis corniculata is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in herbal medicine to support the treatment of various ailments, particularly those related to the digestive system.
